Name: The Purple Apron Food Pantry
Hours:
Check @hcpurpleapron on Instagram for current schedule. Room B103, Lower Level of West Building
Monday: 1:00–6:30 pm
Tuesday: 2:30–6:30 pm
Wednesday: 1:00–6:30 pm
Thursday: 2:30–6:30 pm
Friday: 9:00 am–1:30 pm
Saturday: 10:00 am–2:30 pm
Eligibility: All currently enrolled CUNY students with valid CUNY ID. If you have questions about this program, email Raquel Torres at rt2049@hunter.cuny.edu.

Name: Dean of Students Emergency Fund
Eligibility: Must be enrolled in a matriculated program and currently in classes; emergencies include job loss, homelessness, eviction, medical issues, death in family

Name: Counseling & Wellness Services
Eligibility: All currently enrolled Hunter students

Name: Mental Health (crisis line)
Eligibility: All CUNY students

Name: Financial Aid Office
Hours: Contact office / Virtual Front Desk available
Eligibility: Matriculated Hunter students

Name: Immigrant Student Success Center
Eligibility: Undocumented, DACA, TPS, and immigrant Hunter students

Name: CUNY Citizenship Now!
Eligibility: All CUNY students and their families

Name: Living@Hunter Residence Life
Campus: Brookdale (25 St), Midtown East (51 St), Hunter's Apartments (79 St)
Eligibility: Full-time matriculated Hunter students; undergrad 2.0+ GPA, grad 3.0+ GPA

Name: NYC Housing Connect (Affordable Housing Lotteries)
Eligibility: NYC residents meeting income requirements

Name: Pantry Link Smart Vending Machines
Hours: Any time Silberman campus is open (24/7 access advantage)
Eligibility: All Hunter students: scan QR code, take a short survey, receive points for food
URL: nycfoodpolicy.org (run by Hunter College NYC Food Policy Center)

Name: CUNY SNAP Assistance
Eligibility: CUNY students who meet federal exemptions (work-study, caregiving, etc.)

Name: Welfare Rights Initiative (WRI)
Eligibility: All Hunter students receiving or seeking public assistance (cash assistance, SNAP, welfare)

Name: Federal Work Study
Eligibility: Students with demonstrated financial need via FAFSA

Name: NYC WELL
Eligibility: All NYC residents

Name: The Trevor Project (LGBTQ+)
Eligibility: LGBTQ+ youth

Name: HCAP Mental Health Program
Eligibility: Asian American & Pacific Islander students and other high-need students

Name: NYC CITYFEPS (Eviction Prevention)
Eligibility: Low-income NYC renters facing eviction

Name: Healthy CUNY Housing Resources
Eligibility: All CUNY students experiencing housing insecurity

Name: CUNY Immigration Assistance Project (CIAP)
Eligibility: CUNY students, faculty, staff impacted by immigration policy changes

Name: Office of AccessABILITY
Eligibility: Students with documented physical, learning, or psychological disabilities
Required Docs: Documentation from physician, psychiatrist, psychologist, or clinical social worker (dated within 5 years)

Name: Student Veteran Services + PROVE Program
Eligibility: Student veterans and military dependents

Name: Children's Learning Center
Eligibility: Hunter students with children

Name: Hunter College AANAPISI Project (HCAP)
Eligibility: AAPI students, first-gen students, English Language Learners, immigrant students

Name: Career Development Services (CDS)
Eligibility: All currently enrolled Hunter students AND alumni
Required Docs: Hunter ID / CUNYfirst login for CareerHunter portal

Name: Rockowitz Writing Center
Campus: 68th St, East Building 7th floor (Silverstein Success Center)
Eligibility: All hunter students
Required Docs: Tutors help with all stages of the writing process including admission and scholarship essays, resumes, and cover letters

Name: Dolciani Math Center
Eligibility: All hunter students

Name: Skirball Science Learning Center
Eligibility: All Hunter students (Chemistry, Biology, Physics, Computer Science)
Required Docs: None: also loans textbooks and study guides for free

Name: Hunter Micro-Internship Program
Eligibility: Current Hunter undergraduate students
Required Docs: Parker Dewey account: Hunter Career Center funds $200 per 10-hour project, up to two projects per student: essentially free paid work experience

Name: CUNY Internship Programs (NYC government jobs)
Eligibility: All currently enrolled CUNY students

Name: JFEW Eleanor Roosevelt Scholars Program
Hours: Women at Hunter College, junior and senior year
Eligibility: Competitive program offering tuition assistance, a paid summer internship in New York, a 3-day Washington DC seminar, dedicated workshops, and invitations to special events at Roosevelt House

Name: Office of Prestigious Scholarships & Fellowships
Hours: All Hunter students: they help you apply for national awards like Fulbright, Boren, Critical Language Scholarship, Watson, etc.
Eligibility: Offers one-on-one review of application essays and teaches personal statement workshops. Hunter students have won virtually every major national and international scholarship

Name: Research Programs with Stipends
Eligibility: Undergraduate students (varies by program)
Required Docs: Hunter has multiple research and mentorship training programs that pay stipends
